description: Inside a bustling coffee shop, Monica and Chandler sit on a sofa, with Joey sitting next to them. Rachel stands behind the sofa, holding a plate.
Rachel holds out the plate and says, "Okay. Chandler, Mon, there's only one banana nut muffin left." Monica and Chandler both reach for it.
Chandler says, "Oh, well, I ordered mine first." Monica grabs the muffin and pulls it toward herself, saying, "Yeah, but I'm so much faster."
Chandler extends his hand toward Monica.
Chandler says, "Give it to me."
Monica holds the muffin away and says, "No." Chandler repeats, "Give it to me."
Chandler leans back on the sofa and says, "Okay, you can have it."
Chandler briefly touches the muffin in Monica's hand, then pulls his hand away as she retains her grip.
Monica licks the top of the muffin, places it on a yellow saucer, and sets it on the table.
Monica pushes the yellow mug and saucer toward Chandler and says, "There, go enjoy your coffee."
Chandler holds the muffin and looks at it.
Chandler takes a huge bite of the muffin and says, "I was there when I got here."
Chandler chews with his mouth full. Phoebe enters the coffee shop and walks toward the group.
Phoebe says, "Hey, you guys. You will never guess who's coming to New York." Chandler points at her while chewing and says, "Quick, Phoebe. Tell us before you can swallow."
Phoebe gestures enthusiastically and says, "Okay. Ryan, that guy I went out with who's in the Navy."
Rachel touches Joey's shoulder.
Rachel asks, "You went out with a guy in the Navy?"
Phoebe answers, "Yeah, I met him when I was playing guitar in Washington Square Park. Ryan threw in"
Phoebe continues, "saltwater taffy because he didn't have any change."
Joey asks, "Hey, is that when you rode Saltwater Taffy Man?" Phoebe replies, "No."
Phoebe shakes her head while gesturing with her hands.
Phoebe holds a white mug and says, "He is my submarine guy. He resurfaces, like, every couple of years."
Phoebe continues, "And we have the most amazing three days together. Only"
Phoebe smiles and says, "this time, he's coming for two weeks. Two whole weeks."
Phoebe nods and smiles.
Phoebe says, "Which means yay."
Monica and Chandler look up at Phoebe.
Rachel asks, "Wait. This guy goes down for like, two years at a time?"
Chandler raises his eyebrows and pumps his fist in a triumphant gesture while looking toward Monica.
Chandler looks at Monica and says, "That'll teach you to lick my muffin."
Chandler continues chewing with a satisfied expression. Ross enters through the door and approaches the group.
Ross says, "Hi."
Ross stops near the sofa.
Rachel asks, "Oh, no. What happened?" Ross replies, "I just spoke to Carol. Ben's got the chickenpox."
The group looks concerned.
An unidentified voice says, "Chickenpox." Ross continues, "Yeah, so if you haven't already had it, chances are you're going to get it." Rachel says, "Well, I've had it."
Monica says, "Yeah, I've had it." Joey says, "Had it, had it." Phoebe says, "Well, I haven't ever had it. I feel so left out. Oh, look," while glancing down at her arm.
Phoebe stares at her arm, then gasps and covers her mouth with her hand.
The screen is completely black, displaying white words: "CREATED BY DAVID CRANE & MARTA KAUFFMAN". The setting then shifts to a city skyline over a river at sunset.
Now inside a bedroom, Monica opens a pink door and enters wearing a red robe. Richard stands near a bed, wearing a dark blue robe.
Monica smiles and says, "Honey, you made the bed again." The words "SUPERVISING PRODUCER MICHAEL BORKOW" appear over the scene.
Monica continues, "I told you, you don't have to do that. This isn't camp." The words "PRODUCED BY TODD STEVENS" appear on screen.
Richard puts his hands in his robe pockets and says, "Oh, then I guess the panty raid last night was totally uncalled for."
Monica smiles at him.
Richard says, "Okay, I am going to take a shower. And today I will be singing Jim Croce's Leroy Brown." The names "BETSY BORNS" and "ALEXA JUNGE" are shown with producer titles.
Richard walks past Monica and exits the room. Monica watches him leave with a smile.
Monica moves to the bed, pulls back the comforter and sheets, and quickly crawls onto the mattress, lying flat on her stomach. Producer credits for Adam Chase and Ira Ungerleider appear.
Richard's voice calls from off-screen, "Monica."
Monica looks toward the open door.
Richard returns to the doorway and asks, "Hey, Mon. I have a question. Is Leroy the baddest man in the whole damn town"
Richard continues, "or the fattest man in the whole" The words "WRITTEN BY BROWN MANDELL" appear.
Richard finishes, "damn town?" The words "DIRECTED BY MICHAEL LEMBECK" are shown.
Monica remains lying face down on the mattress.
Monica turns her head toward him and says, "The baddest. Otherwise, the song would be Fat, Fat Leroy Brown."
Richard smiles.
Richard asks, "What are you doing?"
Monica shifts her position slightly on the bed.
Monica rests her chin on her hands and says, "I'm just waiting for you, sweetie."
Monica rests her cheek on the mattress and smiles.
In a bedroom, Monica fluffs a duvet on the bed while Richard, wearing a robe, watches her from the doorway.
Richard asks, "Are you remaking the" and Monica interrupts, "Sorry," holding her hands up with palms facing outward.
Sitting down on the bed, Monica says, "I'm sorry? You know what? The way you did it, it was just fine."
Richard steps into the room, adjusting his robe. "Then you're redoing it," he says. Monica replies, "Because if I tell you, you'll think I'm crazy."
Richard says, "You're pretty much running that risk either way."
Richard sits down on the edge of the bed beside her.
Monica stands, points at the bedding, and says, "Okay. You see, the duvet tag shouldn't be at the top left corner. It should be at the"
Monica gestures to the foot of the bed and finishes, "bottom right corner."
Richard smiles and says, "Well, that's not so crazy." Monica walks to the other side of the bed and says, "I'm just easing you in. All right. All right. You see these little flower"
Pointing to the pattern on the duvet, Monica says, "blossoms? They should be facing up, not down because, well, the head of the bed is where the sun would be." She holds up her fist.
Monica keeps her fist raised. Richard leans forward on the bed, resting his weight on his elbows.
Looking at Monica, Richard says, "You don't love me anymore, do you?"
Monica smiles at him and lowers her gaze slightly.
Looking back at him, Monica says, "Actually, if it's possible, I love you more."
Richard maintains his gaze on Monica.
Richard asks, "Really?"
Monica nods and gestures toward the door.
Monica says, "Wow. Well, then, come on. I want to show you how to fold a toilet paper into a point." She walks away as Richard gets up to follow her.
The scene changes to a busy city street filled with traffic.

The scene changes to an office. Chandler sits at a desk while Joey sits in a chair across from him. Chandler says, "Hey, look, Joey, I'm just saying if you need something to hold you over I can get you a job right here as an entry level processor."
Joey asks, "But don't you need experience for a job like that?" Chandler replies, "It's not that hard to learn. And as for people realizing that you have no idea what you're doing."
Gesturing with a pen, Chandler continues, "Hey, you're an actor. Act like a processor. People will think you're a processor."
Scott walks through the office doorway holding a folder.
Scott steps up to the desk and hands Chandler the folder, saying, "Hey, Chandler. Here's this morning's projection." Chandler takes it and says, "Hey, thanks. Scott Alexander, Joey Tribbiani."
Chandler lowers his pen.
Chandler points his pen at Joey and says, "Joey is a fellow." Scott smiles and says, "No kidding?"
Joey stands up from his chair.
Joey says, "Oh, yeah."
Joey nods and begins to raise his arms.
Crossing his arms, Joey says, "I process."
Gesturing with his hand, Joey says, "People want the processing, I'm the one they call."
Scott asks, "Where do you work?" Joey gestures with one hand and answers, "Well, right now I'm in between things."
Joey points his finger as he speaks. "You know how it is. One day you're processing, the next day you're not so much processing."
Chandler says, "I was just telling Joey about the opening in Fleischmann's group." Scott laughs slightly and says, "Fleischmann's group?"
Scott points back at Chandler as he walks toward the door, saying, "Whatever you do, don't touch his sandwiches."
Joey and Chandler laugh as Scott exits. Joey's smile then fades.
Joey looks at Chandler and asks, "Are all you processors dorks?"
The scene changes to an exterior shot of a rainy city intersection with pedestrians and cars.
Inside an apartment kitchen, Phoebe sits at a table with white spots of cream covering her face. Rachel stands beside her, applying makeup, while Monica watches.
Rachel continues applying makeup to Phoebe and says, "This lipstick looks just great on you."
Monica steps closer to Phoebe.
Monica leans in and says, "You look fabulous, honey. You really do."
Phoebe looks at them and asks, "Yeah. Are you sure? Really?"
Phoebe picks up a wooden hand mirror from the table and looks at her reflection.
Rachel says, "See? You look beautiful." Monica, looking closely at Phoebe, mutters, "For God's sakes, dim the light?"
Phoebe lowers the mirror slightly, her mouth falling open.
Phoebe says, "I'm hideous," her voice trembling slightly. Monica touches her shoulder and says, "It's gonna be okay."
Rachel tilts her head while looking at Phoebe.
Rachel says, "Ryan's been underwater."
Leaning toward Phoebe, Rachel adds, "He's just gonna be so glad that you don't have barnacles on your butt."
Phoebe places the mirror face down on the table. The scene changes to an exterior view of a brick apartment building at night.
In a hallway, Ryan, wearing a white Navy uniform and carrying a duffel bag and a bouquet of flowers, approaches a door.
Ryan stops at the green door marked "20" and knocks twice.
Ryan turns the doorknob and pushes the door open.
Stepping inside, Ryan says, "Hey, baby, I'm back." Phoebe peers out from behind the door, keeping most of her body hidden, and replies, "Hey, Ryan. What's up?"
Phoebe grips the edge of the door, keeping it between them.
Inside an apartment, Ryan, still wearing his white Navy uniform and holding a bouquet, walks forward. By the window, Phoebe stands with a sheer pink veil covering her head and face.
Ryan steps into the living room area. Holding her hands up with palms out, Phoebe says, "What's going? No, you know, you have to stay back. I have the pox."
Ryan stops, his arm lowering as he stares at her.
Ryan asks, "Chicken or small?"
Phoebe stands motionless.
Phoebe answers, "Chicken. Which is so ironic, considering I'm a vegetarian."
Ryan gestures with one hand toward her.
"Why aren't you at home in bed?" Ryan asks. Phoebe replies, "Because my grandmother's never had chickenpox. Please tell me you have, because"
Phoebe continues, "oh, my God. I forgot how cute you are." Ryan shifts his stance and says, "I'm sorry. I never had them."
Ryan exhales loudly, closing his eyes briefly and shaking his head.
Raising one finger, Ryan says, "If I had, one wish would be to build a time machine. Go back to when I was seven. When Jimmy House had the chickenpox. I would grab that kid"
Gesturing emphatically near his own face, Ryan continues, "and rub him all over my"
Phoebe gestures with her hands.
Phoebe says, "or. You know, you could just wish that I didn't have them now."
Ryan tilts his head slightly.

Ryan asks, "Can I please see your face?" Phoebe replies, "No. You don't want to see a face covered with pox." Gesturing with his bouquet hand, Ryan says, "Your face could be covered with lox, I wouldn't care."
Phoebe touches her chest.
Phoebe says, "And you hate fish," her volume softer than her previous lines.
Ryan looks at her, his lips pressed together.
Phoebe says, "Sweet."
Ryan nods, saying, "All right. Okay."
"All right. You can see," Phoebe says.
Phoebe raises both hands and grasps the bottom edges of the pink veil.

Saying "This is me," Phoebe throws the veil back over her head, revealing her face dotted with white spots. Thunder crashes audibly as lightning flashes outside the window, illuminating her face brightly.
Ryan screams loudly, his mouth wide open, and steps back rapidly. Phoebe immediately screams loudly in response, her eyes widening.
Ryan holds one hand up, panting heavily. The lighting returns to its normal indoor level.
Ryan gestures with one hand and says, "Sorry. The lightning. Lightning was an unfortunate coincidence."
Ryan lowers his hand.
Sweeping his arm toward her, Ryan says, "You look lovely."
"Lovely. I hate this," Phoebe says. She gestures widely and asks, "Can I tell you, I had the most amazing two weeks planned for us? And almost"
Pulling the veil from her shoulder, Phoebe continues, "everything I had in mind. We had to be a lot closer than this."
Ryan touches his forehead.
Gesturing downward, Ryan says, "Phoebe, I have spent the last eight months in a steel tube with men"
Ryan pauses, his gaze remaining steady on Phoebe.
Ryan points downward again, saying, "thinking about this moment."
Ryan takes a step forward.
Ryan says, "I am not going to let a bunch of itchy spots stand between us," while looking directly at her.
Ryan steps onto the low coffee table, crosses it rapidly, and jumps down right in front of Phoebe. He grabs her arms, pulling her close.
Ryan kisses Phoebe deeply. Phoebe holds onto his arms. Audience laughter is audible.
Pulling back slightly, Phoebe says, "Okay? This is the most romantic disease I've ever had," her voice slightly shaky.
Ryan touches Phoebe's face.
Ryan leans in again to kiss her as she says, "Oh, good."

The setting changes to a city exterior showing tall buildings, then to an office. Chandler, wearing a suit, sits at a desk typing on a computer keyboard. Joey, wearing a dark suit, opens the door and walks in.

Chandler stops typing and asks, "Hey, how's the first day going?" Joey replies, "Pretty good. It's like you said. It's mostly just putting numbers from one column into another column," gesturing with his hands.
Chandler nods, saying, "Well, there you go." Joey says, "Hey."
Joey gestures outward and says, "And everybody is so nice."
Joey points his thumb back toward the door, saying, "I just had a good talk with that lady with the red hair." Chandler asks, "Jeannie?"
Chandler raises his eyebrows and says, "Jeannie. The head of East Coast operations. Jeannie."
Joey smiles, extending his arms slightly, and says, "Yeah. Turns out our kids go to the same school."
Chandler stares at Joey, his mouth slightly open. Audience laughter is audible. Joey maintains a broad smile.
Joey grabs the lapels of his suit jacket and says, "Yeah. Small world, huh?"
Chandler keeps his gaze fixed on Joey.
Chandler says, "Weird world."
Joey looks at Chandler, his expression neutral.
"Your kids?" Chandler asks.
Joey shrugs slightly, extending one hand, and answers, "I figure my character has kids."
Chandler rubs his mouth with his hand, his eyes wide.
Joey tilts his head and says, "You know?"
Chandler says, "There isn't a part of that sentence that I don't need explained," shaking his head slightly.
Joey steps closer to the desk.
Joey gestures with both hands as he explains, "Well, see, when you're acting, you need to think about stuff like that. My character, Joseph, the processing guy,"
Joey continues, pointing downward, "has two little girls, Ashley and Brittany."
Joey chuckles and waves one hand downward.
Still smiling, Joey says, "Ashley copies everything Brittany does."
Joey continues smiling as Chandler stares at him, lips pressed tight, his eyes slightly narrowed. Audience laughter continues until the clip ends.
In an office setting, Joey stands wearing a dark suit and blue tie, looking off-screen.
Off-screen, Chandler says, "Well, invisible kids can be that way sometimes." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey raises his hands slightly.
Joey gestures with open hands, saying, "You know, Joseph and his wife, Karen, are thinking of having a third kid. You know what?"
Joey lowers his hands.
Joey points a finger upward and says, "Just did."
The scene changes to Chandler sitting at a desk.
Chandler says, "Really? Wow, that's some pretty powerful imaginary sperm you must have"
Chandler says, "there." The scene shows Joey standing with his arms slightly outstretched before lowering them.
The scene changes to the exterior of an apartment building on a city street corner.
The scene changes to the interior of an apartment, showing hands moving game pieces on a Monopoly board.
Phoebe and Ryan sit on the floor around a coffee table with the Monopoly board between them. Both have patches of white lotion on their faces.
Looking at Phoebe, Ryan says, "What makes the itching even worse is that you don't stop talking about it." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe nods slightly.
Phoebe says, "Fine. Let's just play."
Ryan nods and says, "Okay? Good."
Phoebe rubs her hands together and says, "Okay, here we go. Double sixes. Here we go."
Phoebe crosses her arms and rubs them rapidly, scratching herself.
Phoebe says, "Here we go. Come to mama."
Ryan points at her.

Holding her hands near her stomach, Phoebe says, "Getting ready to roll the dice." Pointing at her, Ryan asks, "What are you doing? Are you scratching?" Phoebe replies, "No." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe continues rubbing her stomach area.
Phoebe says, "This is what I do for luck, okay?"
Ryan extends his hand toward her.
Ryan points at her and says, "You're scratching. Give me the dice."
Phoebe says, "No." Ryan extends his hand and says, "Give me the dice." Phoebe hands them over, saying, "Here. There. Double sixes."
Ryan holds the dice in his hand.
Ryan says, "We can't scratch. You know we can't. We'll scar."
Phoebe shakes her head.
Phoebe gestures with both hands over the game board and says, "I can't stop thinking about it. It's so hard. Just want to grab all these houses and rub them all over my body."
Ryan reaches across the table, gripping her hands, and says, "No."
Phoebe pulls at her hands.
Pulling on his hands, Phoebe says, "Come on, you know you want it."
Still gripping his hands and shaking them, Phoebe says, "You know you want it, too. Come on, let's just be bad. It'll feel so good."
Ryan exhales and closes his eyes, tilting his head back slightly.
Phoebe says, "Come on."
Ryan opens his eyes.
Ryan tilts his head back again and says, "God help me."
Phoebe moves to Ryan's side and vigorously rubs his chest with both hands.
Ryan says, "Yeah. Oh, yeah," as Phoebe continues rubbing his chest.
Phoebe turns her back to him and says, "Okay, good. Now do me. Do my back. Come on. Harder. There you go. Don't move," while Ryan vigorously rubs her back with both hands.

They sit on the floor back-to-back, moving their bodies rapidly against each other to scratch. A man in a suit and a woman in a dress walk through the front door and stop, staring at them. Loud audience laughter is audible.
The man and woman stand frozen in the doorway. Phoebe and Ryan stop moving.
Phoebe says, "It right now."
The man in the doorway gestures toward them.
The man says, "You know, I might have expected this of you, Phoebe, but, Ryan, you're a military man."
Ryan quickly stands up. Audience laughter is audible.
Ryan points at Phoebe and says, "She made me do it."
Phoebe gasps and stands up.
Phoebe points at Ryan and says, "That's a nice color on you." Ryan steps toward her and replies, "Yeah, well, it comes in pink and blotchy, baby."
Phoebe stares at Ryan.
Phoebe says, "I hope you get a weenie pock." Audience laughter is audible.
The man in the doorway quickly covers the woman's ears with his hands. The scene changes to an office corridor.
Joey stands facing a woman holding a blue folder. Chandler leans against a water cooler in the background. Joey says, "You and Milton have to join us on the boat."
Joey gestures and says, "Karen will pack a lunch."
Joey says, "You'll bring the kids. We'll make a day of it." The woman smiles and says, "That sounds lovely. We're gonna have to set that up. Oh, I better get back. Hope the baby"
The woman says, "feels better." Joey nods.
The woman turns away and says, "Thanks. Bye." Joey says, "Bye, Jeannie."
The woman says, "Bye bye," as she walks away.
Joey turns to face Chandler.
Joey points his thumb in the woman's direction and says, "What a phony." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler steps forward from the water cooler and approaches Joey.
Chandler says, "Well, I'm sure you'll teach her a lesson when she steps off the dock onto nothing."
A man wearing a tie approaches Joey, holding a folder.
The man stops and says, "Hey, Mr. Douglas, sir."
Joey turns toward the man.
Joey says, "Listen."
Joey reaches for and takes the folder from the man. The screen cuts to black at 11:13.
The screen remains black and the audio stops, continuing in silence until the segment ends.
